behavior_summary: This script implements an item-bound 'U8P_EGHOUL::hatch' process for an egg-like item. When the process runs for a valid item instance it delegates to a helper (U8P_EGHOUL::func00BD) which advances a frame/progress counter on the item until a threshold is reached, at which point it attempts to spawn a creature (NPC) at the item's location and then removes the item.

Progression path: read item's frame (Item::getFrame). If below a threshold it increments the frame (Item::setFrame), spawns a timed helper (spawn ->FREE::func1C19) and yields (pid/suspend) so growth continues later. If threshold reached, push the item (Item::push), try to create the NPC (Npc::create), on success pop coordinates into the NPC (Item::pop with Z,Y,X,npc), set the NPC's target to the player (Npc::setTarget) and destroy the item (Item::destroy). If NPC creation fails the item is still popped/destroyed as cleanup.
The script is robust to spawn failure: it still removes the item even when Npc::create fails.
notable_control_flow: U8P_EGHOUL::hatch is a process entry that only acts when the process context/object ('this') is present; its sole effect is to call U8P_EGHOUL::func00BD.

U8P_EGHOUL::func00BD implements a two-branch state machine: advance-and-wait branch (increment frame, spawn timed helper, suspend) and finalize branch (create NPC, place it, set target, destroy item).
Yielding behavior via pid/suspend is used after scheduling the timed helper so the U8P_EGHOUL::hatch process resumes later rather than spinning in-place.
open_questions: Exact numeric U8P_EGHOUL::hatch threshold (evidence suggests 0x07 but this is inferred from decompilation remnants).
Exact NPC type id created by Npc::create (inferred as 0x008E from comments but not canonicalized in metadata).
Timing semantics of the spawned helper (spawn ->FREE::func1C19 called with 0) — how long until the next resume is not explicit here.
Whether Item::push/pop also perform locking/reservation beyond coordinate retrieval (inferred from usage pattern).

main_function.name: U8P_EGHOUL::hatch
main_function.purpose: Process-level entry that ensures the item/context exists and delegates U8P_EGHOUL::hatch lifecycle handling to the helper function U8P_EGHOUL::func00BD.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine as an item-bound process (processType 0x0210).
Runs only when the associated item/context ('this') is present/truthy; otherwise it does nothing.

function_map.name: U8P_EGHOUL::hatch
function_map.role: process entry for the item; guard + delegator

function_map.name: U8P_EGHOUL::func00BD
function_map.role: implements U8P_EGHOUL::hatch progression, scheduling, NPC creation/placement, and item cleanup

internal_calls.purpose: Delegate the U8P_EGHOUL::hatch lifecycle processing for the current item/context.

external_calls.purpose: Read the item's current frame/progress index to decide whether to advance or finalize hatching.
Write back an incremented frame/progress index when advancing U8P_EGHOUL::hatch state.
Schedule a timed/background helper so the U8P_EGHOUL::hatch process continues later (delay/resume mechanism).
Yield the current U8P_EGHOUL::hatch process so the scheduled helper can run before further progress.
Prepare or reserve the item's coordinates (and/or protect the item) before attempting NPC placement.
Attempt to create the hatched creature (returns success/failure and an NPC handle).
On success: pop Z,Y,X into the created NPC to place it at the item's location; on failure: pop/cleanup the item reference.
Set the newly created NPC's target (0x01 likely denotes the player/avatar).
Remove the item from the world after hatching or after failed NPC creation (cleanup).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements an item-bound 'U8P_EGHOUL::hatch' process for an egg-like item. When the process runs for a valid item instance it delegates to a helper (U8P_EGHOUL::func00BD) which advances a frame/progress counter on the item until a threshold is reached, at which point it attempts to spawn a creature (NPC) at the item's location and then removes the item.
